combat_history.event: Cavour deployed to the Mediterranean during NATO intervention in Libya, operating AV-8B Harrier II aircraft in support of coalition operations
combat_history.significance: First operational deployment demonstrating Italy's carrier aviation capability in combat operations

combat_history.event: First F-35B Lightning II landings and takeoffs from Cavour during trials off the US East Coast, marking Italy's transition to fifth-generation naval aviation
combat_history.significance: Historic milestone transforming Italy into one of few nations operating stealth fighters from aircraft carriers

combat_history.event: Large-scale NATO exercise in the Mediterranean with Cavour serving as flagship, operating both F-35Bs and helicopters simultaneously
combat_history.significance: Demonstrated full operational capability with F-35B integration complete

modernization.description: Flight deck modifications including heat-resistant coatings, reinforced landing areas, upgraded fuel systems, and enhanced maintenance facilities for F-35B operations
modernization.impact: Transformed Cavour from helicopter carrier to true fixed-wing aircraft carrier with fifth-generation fighter capability

modernization.description: Expected upgrades to radar systems, combat management systems, and potentially propulsion efficiency improvements
modernization.impact: Will extend operational life and maintain technological relevance through 2040s

vulnerabilities.description: 16-knot maximum speed significantly limits tactical flexibility and ability to generate wind-over-deck for aircraft operations in low wind conditions
vulnerabilities.context: Modern anti-ship missiles and submarines require higher speeds for effective evasion, and slow speed complicates flight operations
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Italy relies on escort vessels and geographic positioning rather than speed for protection

vulnerabilities.description: Maximum of 8-10 fixed-wing aircraft severely limits sustained air operations compared to larger carriers
vulnerabilities.context: Limited sortie generation rate reduces effectiveness in high-intensity conflicts
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Focus on high-quality F-35B aircraft and coordination with land-based assets

vulnerabilities.description: Only 32 SAM cells and no long-range air defense systems make Cavour heavily dependent on escorts for protection
vulnerabilities.context: Vulnerable to saturation attacks or advanced anti-ship missiles without strong escort screen
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Always operates within escort groups and relies on allied air cover

operational_patterns.typical_deployment: Mediterranean patrol, NATO exercises, expeditionary operations as flagship of task groups
operational_patterns.readiness_notes: Generally high availability with approximately 200+ sea days annually

peer_comparisons.platform: Juan Carlos I Strategic Projection Ship
peer_comparisons.key_differences: Juan Carlos I larger with better amphibious capability but Cavour has superior sensors and dedicated carrier design. Both operate F-35Bs but Cavour has better aviation fuel capacity.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Mediterranean carrier rivalry - comparing Italian naval aviation focus vs Spanish amphibious flexibility

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Charles de Gaulle much larger with nuclear propulsion and catapult launch system enabling larger aircraft, but Cavour more affordable and easier to maintain with modern F-35B technology.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: European carrier philosophy - nuclear catapult vs conventional STOVL approach

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Queen Elizabeth much larger with 40+ F-35B capacity vs Cavour's 8-10, but Cavour has better point defense and more mature operational doctrine.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Post-Brexit European naval power - comparing UK ambitions vs Italian pragmatism

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Similar F-35B integration timeline but Izumo larger with better helicopter capacity. Cavour has superior sensors and combat systems while Izumo focuses on ASW.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: F-35B revolution - how medium powers are gaining carrier aviation capability
